title: Show Me!
description: Show Me! is a sex education by Helga Fleischhauer-Hardt. The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976.
descriptionParagraphs: Show Me! is a sex education by Helga Fleischhauer-Hardt. The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976.

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a sex education, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976. The German-language edition was classified as objectionable by the OFLC in 1996: "the book purports to be a manual for the sexual education of. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Show Me! is a sex education by Helga Fleischhauer-Hardt. The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976.
banningParagraphs: Show Me! entered censorship debates as a sex education associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity and public morality.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1976 in New Zealand, where Indecent Publications Tribunal classified, prohibited, or restricted. The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976. The German-language edition was classified as objectionable by the OFLC in 1996: "the book purports to be a manual for the sexual education of. The English-language edition was ruled indecent by the IPT in 1976. The German-language edition was classified as objectionable by the OFLC in 1996: "the book purports to be a manual for the sexual education of children, but presents children in an erotic.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
